rulings.question: Did prior breadth make keep-create-changelog conclusive?
rulings.ruling: No. It kept create-changelog only at medium confidence and grounded the case in the template/output lifecycle, not in sk-doc-owned workflow doctrine.
rulings.rationale: The prior packet-boundary finding cited changelog_template.md lines for output location and GitHub release-note use, so it left ownership unresolved for this depth pass.

rulings.question: Does changelog_template.md carry enough workflow doctrine to justify a standalone sk-doc workflow packet?
rulings.ruling: No. It is a shared format/style asset, not the workflow owner.
rulings.rationale: The template says it is a copy-paste starting point for /create:changelog and human authors, and it explicitly sends nested packet-local changelogs to system-spec-kit templates instead.

rulings.question: Does /create:changelog YAML contain real command-owned logic?
rulings.ruling: Yes. The real workflow owner is create_changelog_auto.yaml.
rulings.rationale: The YAML defines autonomous execution, the seven-step workflow, component resolution, version calculation, content generation, validation, writing, and reporting.

rulings.question: Should create-changelog remain one of the sk-doc workflow packets?
rulings.ruling: No. Fold changelog_template.md into shared/assets and point the command at it; do not create a dedicated create-changelog packet unless workflow ownership is moved from /create:changelog into sk-doc.
rulings.rationale: A standalone packet would either be a shell around the template or duplicate the command YAML, because the YAML already owns detection, routing, versioning, validation, and write behavior.

rulings.question: Is there contract drift to fix during the fold?
rulings.ruling: Yes. Reconcile template/YAML header expectations before moving paths.
rulings.rationale: The template says changelog files start directly with the summary and have no version header, while the YAML validation still checks for an H1 version title and version/date header.

risks: Asset-path risk: create_changelog_auto.yaml currently hardcodes .opencode/skills/sk-doc/assets/changelog_template.md, so folding requires updating that command pointer. Evidence: .opencode/commands/create/assets/create_changelog_auto.yaml:252-280

Validation drift risk: template and command disagree on header format, so a path-only fold can preserve a broken contract. Evidence: .opencode/skills/sk-doc/assets/changelog_template.md:38-40; .opencode/commands/create/assets/create_changelog_auto.yaml:533-542
open: Confirm-mode parity remains open: changelog_template.md says create_changelog_confirm.yaml also reads this template, but this pass only inspected the requested auto YAML. Evidence: .opencode/skills/sk-doc/assets/changelog_template.md:28
